item.title: 在我的职业生涯开始时我会对自己说的话 | What I would say to myself at the beginning of my career
item.description:
几天前，我遇到了一个问题，根据我的经验，我会对开始从事前端开发的人说些什么。 这让我开始思考自从成为开发人员以来我的思维方式发生了什么变化。

我承认这是一个很难回答的问题，因为我不仅作为一个专业人士而且作为一个人都发生了很大的变化——对我来说，一件事是相互联系的。 但是，我想强调一些想法，我认为如果 15 年前就知道这些想法就太好了，我希望现在能对您有所帮助。

我之前想得到的一些建议

我记得当我进入 IT 领域时我是多么着迷，我想知道多少事情才能成为一名优秀的网页设计师。 在某些时候，这些感觉令人难以置信，因为它激励我学习很多东西，然而，很多时候它们也让我感到沮丧和对自己严厉。 我在设计和前端代码之间左右为难，并且给自己施加了太大的压力，要同时学习这两者并变得出色。

所以我的建议是放轻松，尽情享受旅程。 拥有一条能为您提供实现目标的必要动力的道路，但也要记住在您的职业生涯开始时尝试一次学习一件事并理解知识需要时间来获得，让自己放松。

与前面的主题完全相关，有时我们会陷入与他人比较时感觉不够好的陷阱，这会导致焦虑和失望。 这种行为在 IT 中非常普遍，直到现在我都必须小心不要这样做，但现在经验帮助我更好地处理这种情况。

了解每个人都是不同的并且有自己的旅程，当您因将自己与他人进行比较而感到沮丧时，请尝试将其作为继续向前看的灵感和燃料。 看看你自己，你的成就并庆祝它。

作为职业生涯早期的开发人员，我记得有多少次我不仅在专业方面，而且在个人方面都想承担更多的风险。 我留下了多少疑虑、项目和网络，因为害怕无法成为一个好的沟通者、浪费我的时间、没有有趣的事情要说。 很多次我破坏了自己。

做你自己，使用你拥有的工具并尝试与他们建立桥梁。 如果失败，请重试。 不要让恐惧麻痹你。 你可以克服它。

当我回顾我的 IT 旅程时我在想什么

经验丰富的我当然可以给新手很多建议，但这并不意味着我后悔，相反，我认识到这些年的经验所采取的每一步，以及作为当今前端开发人员的成就感。

有很多事情我可以庆祝，比如通过帮助组织前端活动积极参与网络社区，为我工作的公司带来趋势并帮助他们改变一些流程，领导团队，在国外作为开发人员生活和工作......这么多很棒的东西！

结论

我们总是能够回顾过去，找出可以减少错误、恐惧和焦虑的不同之处，但重要的是要强调，所有这一切对于打造今天的我们和明天想要成为的人都是必要的。 通过我们自己的经验，我们也可以帮助其他人走上他们自己的旅程。 多么神奇的事情，不是吗？

我希望这篇文章能帮助您对自己感到自在，并记得从容应对您的旅程。

下次见。 😁 😁



原文:

A few days ago I came across a question about what I would say to someone who is starting in frontend development based on my experience. And it got me thinking about what has changed in my mindset since becoming a developer.

I confess that it's a difficult question to answer because I've changed a lot not only as a professional but also as a person - and for me one thing is linked to the other. However, there are a few thoughts I would like to highlight that I think it would have been great to have known 15 years ago and I hope can help you now.

Some advises I would like to have before

1 - Stop trying to be a perfect professional

I remember how fascinated I was when I entered the IT field and how many things I wanted to know to become a great web designer. At some point these feelings were incredible because it motivated me to study a lot, however, many times they also left me frustrated and severe with myself. I was torn between design and front-end code and was putting too much pressure on myself to learn both at the same time and become great at it.

So my suggestion is to take it easy and enjoy the journey a lot. Have a path that gives you the necessary motivation to reach your goals, but also remember to be at ease with yourself trying to learn one thing at a time in the beginning of your career and understanding that knowledge takes time to be obtained.

2 - People are different, so don’t compare yourself to others

Totally connected to the previous topic, sometimes we can fall into the trap of not feeling good enough when we compare ourselves to others, which can lead to anxiety and disappointment. This behaviour is super common in IT and until now I had to be careful not to do it, but experience has helped me to deal with this situation better nowadays.

Understanding that each person is different and has their own journeys, when you get upset about comparing yourself to others, try to make it an inspiration and fuel to keep looking forward. Look at yourself, your achievements and celebrate it.

3 - Don’t be afraid to failure, just do it

As a developer early in my career, I remember how many times I wanted to take more risks not only in terms of professional, but also as a person. How many doubts, projects and networking did I leave behind for fear of failing as a good communicator, of wasting my time, of not having interesting things to say. Many times I sabotaged myself.

“Go ahead”, I would tell myself in the past. Be yourself, use the tools you have and try to build bridges with them. If it fails, try again. Don't let fear paralyse you. You can overcome it.

What I think of when I look back on my IT journey

My experienced self can certainly advise my novice self a lot, but that doesn't mean I regret it, on the contrary, I recognise every step taken to reach these years of experience and how much I feel accomplished as a frontend developer nowadays.

There are many things I can celebrate, such as actively participating in the web community by helping to organize frontend events, bringing trends to the companies I worked for and helping them to change some processes, leading teams, living and working as a developer abroad… So many awesome things!

Conclusion

We have always been able to look back and identify what could be different to alleviate mistakes, fears and anxieties, but it is important to emphasise that all of this is necessary to build who we are today and who we want to be tomorrow. Through our own experiences, we can also help others on their own journeys. What a magical thing, isn’t it?

I hope this article has helped you feel comfortable with yourself and remember to take your journey in stride.

See you next time. 😁
item.author: diogorodrigues

item.title: React 中的条件包装 - 一种高级方法 | Conditional Wrapping in React - An Advanced Approach
item.description:
在 React 中，有时我们有一个通用元素需要根据特定条件进行不同的渲染。 例如，我们可能有一个显示项目列表的组件，如果每个项目都有特定的 URL，我们希望将其包装在一个 link 元素中。 处理此问题的一种方法是使用 if-else 语句，但这会导致代码混乱和重复。

一种更简洁、更灵活的解决方案是使用高阶组件 ( HOC ) 来为我们处理条件包装。 在本文中，我们将探讨如何使用 HOC 为 React 中的条件包装创建可重用且灵活的解决方案。

首先，让我们创建一个名为withConditionalWrapper的HOC ，它接受一个条件和一个包装器组件，并在满足条件时返回一个将其子组件包装在指定包装器组件中的新组件。 这是withConditionalWrapper的代码：

 const withConditionalWrapper = ( condition , Wrapper ) => WrappedComponent => props => condition ? ( < Wrapper {... props } > < WrappedComponent {... props } / > < /Wrapper > ) : ( < WrappedComponent {... props } / > );

要使用withConditionalWrapper ，我们只需将条件和我们要使用的包装组件传递给它，然后将我们要包装的组件作为参数传递。 例如，如果存在url属性，这里是我们如何使用withConditionalWrapper将我们的ItemList组件包装在一个 link 元素中：

 const ItemListWithConditionalWrapper = withConditionalWrapper ( ({ url }) => !! url , ' a ' )( ItemList );

现在，当我们渲染ItemListWithConditionalWrapper时，如果url属性存在，它会自动将每个项目包装在链接元素中，如果url属性不存在，它将不会被包装。

使用像withConditionalWrapper这样的 HOC 的好处之一是它允许我们在不修改其原始代码的情况下向我们的组件添加额外的功能。 在使用我们无法控制的第三方组件时，这尤其有用。

使用 HOC 进行条件包装的另一个优点是它们具有高度可重用性。 我们可以使用相同的withConditionalWrapper HOC 来根据任何条件使用任何包装器元素包装任何组件。 在处理大型复杂项目时，这可以为我们节省大量时间和精力。

总之，在 React 中使用 HOC 进行条件包装可以为常见问题提供干净、灵活且可重用的解决方案。 无论您是使用自己的组件还是第三方组件，HOC 都可以帮助您在不修改原始代码的情况下添加额外的功能。

最后，我们讨论了使用高阶组件作为 React 中条件包装的高级方法。 此方法允许向包装元素添加其他功能，例如传递道具或应用自定义样式。 虽然Chris Bongers 使用通用组件的技术在某些情况下很有用，但 HOC 为 React 中的高级条件包装提供了更灵活和可定制的解决方案。



原文:

In React, there may be times when we have a generic element that needs to be rendered differently based on a specific condition. For example, we might have a component that displays a list of items, and we want to wrap each item in a link element if it has a specific URL. One way to handle this is to use an if-else statement, but this can result in messy and duplicative code.

A cleaner and more flexible solution is to use higher order components (HOCs) to handle the conditional wrapping for us. HOCs are higher-order functions that take a component and return a new component with additional functionality. In this article, we will explore how to use HOCs to create a reusable and flexible solution for conditional wrapping in React.

First, let's create an HOC called withConditionalWrapper that takes a condition and a wrapper component, and returns a new component that wraps its children in the specified wrapper component if the condition is met. Here is the code for withConditionalWrapper:

const withConditionalWrapper = (condition, Wrapper) => WrappedComponent => props => condition ? ( <Wrapper {...props}> <WrappedComponent {...props} />
 </Wrapper>
 ) : ( <WrappedComponent {...props} />
 );

To use withConditionalWrapper, we simply pass it the condition and the wrapper component we want to use, and then pass the component we want to wrap as an argument. For example, here is how we can use withConditionalWrapper to wrap our ItemList component with a link element if the url prop is present:

const ItemListWithConditionalWrapper = withConditionalWrapper( ({ url }) => !!url, 'a'
)(ItemList);

Now, when we render ItemListWithConditionalWrapper, it will automatically wrap each item in a link element if the url prop is present, and it will not be wrapped if the url prop is not present.

One of the benefits of using an HOC like withConditionalWrapper is that it allows us to add additional functionality to our component without modifying its original code. This can be especially useful when working with third-party components that we do not have control over.

Another advantage of using HOCs for conditional wrapping is that they are highly reusable. We can use the same withConditionalWrapper HOC to wrap any component with any wrapper element based on any condition. This can save us a lot of time and effort when working on large and complex projects.

In conclusion, using HOCs for conditional wrapping in React can provide a clean, flexible, and reusable solution for a common problem. Whether you are working with your own components or third-party components, HOCs can help you add additional functionality without modifying the original code.

To wrap up, we've discussed the use of higher order components as an advanced approach to conditional wrapping in React. This method allows for the addition of additional functionality, such as passing props or applying custom styles, to the wrapped elements. While Chris Bongers' technique of using a generic component can be useful in certain situations, HOCs offer a more flexible and customizable solution for advanced conditional wrapping in React.

item.title: Unixporn 关于错误处理。 | Unixporn on error handling.
item.description:
迈克达姆

发表于你好👋

在过去 25 年对不同的 Linux 桌面进行长期测试之后，我最终选择了 i3wm(gaps)。 通常像我这样的人，将 i3wm 主题化为个人用途，并在名为unixporn的 reddit 上分享

在崩溃或系统切换后，不要从一开始就设置所有东西。 在 GitHub 上保存所谓的 .dotfiles 是很常见的。 在全新安装并且所有设置都恢复后，您可以在本地计算机上克隆它。

但是，Linux 安装本身有什么用呢？ 这个备份在哪里？ 总是安装相同的包、键入相同的命令、配置服务真的很烦人。

所以我决定编写一个自己的安装后设置 (bitslixOS)。 此设置基于 debian-net-nonfree_install 并将安装所有软件包、设置所有内容、克隆您的存储库等。

在计划/测试阶段，我注意到处理和读取错误并不容易。 所以我做了下一个决定 -> 编写一个自己的错误库。

这是一个文件中的独立库，可以在任何脚本上使用，只需通过源文件即可。 错误将被陷阱捕获，之后将完成日志文件中的条目，而不是在控制台上输出。 由于 unixporn，错误输出和日志格式很好，作为奖励，将显示源文件的一部分（错误）。

该库还包括一个调试输出选项。 这可以通过控制台调用的 var 或 fast 选项激活。

很多事情要做，所以请耐心等待。

制造 iptoux。

苏拉杰维斯瓦卡玛 - 一旦暂停，iptoux 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，iptoux 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，iptoux 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 iptoux 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 iptoux 的操作：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 iptoux 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Maik Damm

Posted on Dec 17

Hello there 👋

After a long journey of testing different Linux desktops over the last 25 year's, i finaly landen on i3wm(gaps). I3wm is an manual tiling window manager, the "gaps" is an newer edition of i3wm.

Normally people like me, theme the i3wm to personal usage and share this on an reddit called unixporn

To not set up all things from begin, after an crash or system switch. It is common, to save so called .dotfiles on GitHub. You can than clone this on your local machine, after a fresh install and all settings are back.

But, what is with the Linux installation itself? Where is the backup of this? It is really annoying to install always the same packages, type the same commands, configure services.

So I decided, to write an own Post-Install setup (bitslixOS). This setup based on debian-net-nonfree_install and will install all packages, setup all things, clone your repo and so on.

In the planing/testing phase, i noticed that's not easy to handle and read the errors. So I had done an next decision -> write an own error library.

This is an standalon library in one file, that can be used on ANY script, simply by source the file. An preview is available on GitHub - bash_error_lib.

Errors will be catched by an trap, after that an entry in the log file will be done, than output on console. Because of unixporn, error output and log nice formated, as bonus, a part of source file (error) will be displayed.

The library also includes an debug output option. This can activated by var or fast option on console call.

Many things are to do, so please be patient.

GitHub - bash_error_lib.

Mfg iptoux.

Suraj Vishwakarma - Dec 9

Once suspended, iptoux will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, iptoux will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by iptoux will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If iptoux is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
